title: Trouble Diagnosis — Self-diagnosis Items (using CONSULT)

figures_detected.description: Illustration of the CONSULT diagnostic tool device
CONSULT screen showing NISSAN CONSULT with START and SUB-MODE buttons
CONSULT screen showing SELF-DIAG RESULTS with FAILURE DETECTED RANGE SWITCH and ERASE/PRINT buttons

steps.text: Connect CONSULT connector to vehicle diagnostic connector. The diagnostic connector is in the fuse box.
Touch "START", "CVT", "SELF-DIAG RESULTS" in this order on the CONSULT screen.
Self diagnostic results are displayed on the screen.

content_blocks.text: Note: Take the following precautions when using CONSULT for self-diagnosis of ENGINE and N•CVT in models equipped with ABS.

Connect the vehicle self-diagnostic connector to CONSULT and turn the ignition switch "ON". When the "SELF-DIAG RESULTS" screen is displayed, the ABS warning lamp blinks, the ABS self-diagnosis result screen starts and the fail-safe mode is entered.
The conditions described above occur when self-diagnosis is performed with CONSULT. When each self-diagnosis is completed, always turn ignition switch "OFF" to cancel the fail-safe.

tools_referenced.name: CONSULT diagnostic tool
tools_referenced.usage: Connect to vehicle diagnostic connector (located in fuse box) to perform self-diagnosis of CVT

model_notes: Applicable to models equipped with N•CVT and ABS. Note regarding ABS warning lamp behaviour when using CONSULT for ENGINE and N•CVT self-diagnosis on ABS-equipped models.
